description: Implement the operational simulation engine so llm-sim can persist, execute, inspect, replay, and regression-test authored simulations end to end.

userStories.title: Extend `ApplicationModelConfig` for runtime policy controls
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want `ApplicationModelConfig` to represent handoff, summarization, truncation, retry, and attribution policy so that runtime modules can consume architecture-complete model settings without fallback comments.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/contracts/application-model-config.ts` includes handoff, summarization, truncation, and cost-attribution policy fields from the PRD-recommended shape
`ApplicationModelConfig` still carries retry, backoff, transport, and output-mode fields needed by runtime transport code
`src/contracts/application-model-config.test.ts` covers the added policy surface
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Correct steering overlay priority ordering
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want steering overlay sorting to follow the architecture's scope and priority rules so that runtime merge order matches authored intent.
acceptanceCriteria: `sortSteeringOverlaysForDeterministicMerge()` keeps broader scopes ahead of narrower scopes
Within the same scope, higher `explicitPriority` sorts ahead of lower `explicitPriority`
A stable tie-break field such as `activatedAtSimulationInstant` is added and used when priorities tie
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add authored soft-skill scoping fields
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want `SoftSkillDefinition` to capture goal-family, chain-state, and supporting prompt scoping so that runtime activation can be constrained by authored metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/contracts/soft-skills.ts` adds supporting prompt fragment ids and allowed/prohibited goal-family and chain-state fields
The new fields are additive and keep authored scoping separate from runtime execution logic
`src/contracts/soft-skills.test.ts` covers the added authored fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Replace tool integration metadata with structured boundary unions
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want tool integration metadata to distinguish in-process, MCP, and HTTP boundaries so that tool runtime code can authorize and surface tools without ambiguous metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/contracts/tools.ts` defines a structured union for in-process, MCP, and HTTP integration metadata
The MCP variant captures server id, tool name, and transport mode needed by later runtime code
Contract tests reflect the new union shape and no helper relies on the removed metadata format
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add authored config SQLite repository
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want authored bundles stored behind a real SQLite repository so that the engine and API can load and revise authored configuration without route-local persistence code.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/authored-config-sqlite-repository.ts` reads and upserts authored bundles using the existing schema
The repository exposes revision-aware reads needed by the config API without embedding route logic
Repository tests run against `:memory:` SQLite
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add steering overlay SQLite repository
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want steering overlays persisted in SQLite so that merged runtime steering can be driven by stored overlay state instead of in-memory placeholders.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/steering-overlay-sqlite-repository.ts` supports create, update, disable, and list operations for overlays
Overlay persistence keeps deterministic merge metadata, including the stable activation tiebreaker from WS-00
Repository tests run against `:memory:` SQLite
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add runtime projection SQLite repository
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want runtime projections stored with replace semantics so that engine state can be atomically reloaded and updated between simulation instants.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/runtime-projection-sqlite-repository.ts` reads and replaces runtime projections by `simulationId`
`bigint` values are serialized as strings at the SQL and JSON boundary
Repository tests verify replace semantics and round-tripping in `:memory:` SQLite
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement append-only canonical world event store
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want canonical world events appended through a transactional SQLite store so that committed simulation truth is durable, ordered, and replayable.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/canonical-world-event-sqlite-store.ts` appends canonical events with transactional per-`simulationId` ledger sequence assignment
The ledger remains append-only and supports bounded ledger-window reads for replay
Repository tests cover monotonic per-simulation ordering and transaction safety
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add telemetry SQLite repository
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want runtime telemetry stored in SQLite so that actor, tool, and engine execution can be inspected through queryable records.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/telemetry-sqlite-repository.ts` supports telemetry append and list operations by simulation and window
Telemetry payloads and bigint-like values round-trip safely through SQLite and JSON serialization
Repository tests run against `:memory:` SQLite
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add checkpoint SQLite repository
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want simulation checkpoints persisted in SQLite so that bounded replay and reset operations can start from committed snapshots instead of rebuilding full history.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/checkpoint-sqlite-repository.ts` saves and loads checkpoints by simulation and instant
Checkpoint reads can supply bounded replay starting points without reconstructing full history
Repository tests cover save, latest-load, and explicit-load behavior in `:memory:` SQLite
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add application model config snapshot SQLite repository
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want model-config snapshots stored per simulation so that runtime transport policy can be reproduced and inspected for a given execution run.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/persistence/application-model-config-sqlite-repository.ts` reads and writes model-config snapshots per simulation
The repository persists the expanded policy surface added in WS-00 through application-owned interfaces
The snapshot repository is exported from `src/persistence/index.ts` and `src/index.ts`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement OpenAI Responses transport text mode
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want a real OpenAI SDK-backed transport for text responses so that actor deliberation can run through production transport code instead of stubs.
acceptanceCriteria: `package.json` adds the `openai` dependency and `src/model-adapter/openai-responses-transport.ts`
The transport uses the OpenAI JS SDK for text-mode responses and returns only normalized `DeliberationTransportResult` values
Success-case tests cover request ids, response ids, and token-usage mapping from SDK responses

Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add structured output and tool-call transport modes
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want the OpenAI transport to support structured output and tool-call responses so that runtime deliberation can handle all configured model output modes.
acceptanceCriteria: `OpenAIResponsesTransport` supports `structured` and `tools` output modes from `ApplicationModelConfig.outputMode`
Structured responses and tool-call proposals are normalized into contract-safe result shapes without executing tools
Unit tests cover structured output and tool-call response cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add retry, backoff, and rate-limit normalization to transport
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want transport retries and rate-limit observations normalized through contracts so that runtime budgeting and failure handling can work without SDK-specific glue.
acceptanceCriteria: Retry and backoff behavior are applied from `ApplicationModelConfig`
Rate-limit metadata, retryable SDK failures, and incomplete responses are normalized into contract-safe observations
Transport results remain usable for runtime budget signals with no extra mapping layer
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Load actor authored data and runtime context into the orchestrator
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want a single orchestrator to load actor authored assets and runtime state so that each turn starts from one authoritative execution path.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/actor/actor-runtime-orchestrator.ts` loads actor profile, prompt fragments, tools, chains, runtime state, and active overlays for a turn
The orchestrator interface matches the PRD input/output shape and keeps this loading logic out of `runActorTurnPipeline()`

userStories.title: Merge steering and derive bounded soft-skill activation
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want the orchestrator to derive the same effective steering and soft-skill set that inspection views expose so that execution and inspection stay consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: The orchestrator merges steering using the same merge code that inspection APIs will expose
Active soft skills are derived from authored scope, goal-family, and chain-state constraints with deterministic bounds

userStories.title: Assemble explainable memory retrieval and prompt inputs
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want the orchestrator to assemble explainable memory retrieval and prompt inputs so that actor deliberation is auditable and reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: The orchestrator builds explainable memory retrieval records for a turn
Prompt instructions and input items include prompt fragments, internal dialogue policy, and retrieved memory context

userStories.title: Integrate deliberation gating and staged turn outputs
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want the orchestrator to gate deliberation and stage turn outputs through one path so that reflex-only and model-backed turns produce the same application-owned artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: The orchestrator calls `runActorTurnPipeline()` for reflex handling and invokes the model adapter only when `needsDeliberation` is true
Deliberation proposals are validated and merged into staged world intents, dialogue, memory writes, runtime updates, internal dialogue artifacts, and `ActorTurnTraceTelemetryPayload`

userStories.title: Add simulation engine lifecycle scaffold
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want a `SimulationEngine` with lifecycle entrypoints so that simulation control stops living in contracts and starts living in executable services.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/engine/simulation-engine.ts` and companion types define `start`, `pause`, `resume`, `reset`, and `step` entrypoints
Lifecycle methods load and persist simulation runtime state through repositories instead of route-local logic

userStories.title: Implement pre-actor instant coordination
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want engine step setup to deterministically collect and order ready work so that each simulation instant begins from a consistent world-owned schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: `step()` loads the current runtime snapshot, begins the instant cycle, collects ready world work, and orders it through `SimulationInstantCoordinator` and `orderReadyWorkForInstant()`
World and workflow continuations execute before reactive actors

userStories.title: Apply deterministic world-side mutations and contention resolution
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want world-side mutations resolved inside the engine step so that resource reservation and contention outcomes stay application-owned and deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: The engine applies deterministic world-side updates and routes conflicts through `resolveWorldContentionMutations()`
Only committed world-engine mutations can reserve resources, and actor turns do not advance time directly

userStories.title: Execute actor turns inside the engine step
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want the engine to run ready actors in deterministic order so that actor execution becomes part of one authoritative simulation loop.
acceptanceCriteria: The engine builds the ready actor set and runs actor turns in deterministic order through the orchestrator
Accepted world actions and staged actor artifacts are collected before canonical events are committed

Tests pass

userStories.title: Commit canonical events, telemetry, runtime projections, and checkpoints
userStories.description: As an implementation agent, I want engine step commit and clock advancement to happen in one sealed path so that canonical events, telemetry, and state snapshots are persisted before time moves forward.
acceptanceCriteria: The engine appends canonical events, telemetry, runtime projections, and checkpoints during `step()`
The instant is sealed before the clock advances, and `step()` returns committed event ids plus the next simulation instant
